task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function accurately calculates the median of a list, handles edge cases correctly, and is well-written.
trace: The function `median(nums)` first sorts the input list `nums`. It checks if the length of the list is zero, returning `None` in that case. If the length is odd, it finds and returns the middle value as a float by dividing the index by 2 and rounding down. For even lengths, it calculates and returns the average of the two middle values by adding them and dividing by 2. An edge case handled here is when the list is empty.
